name: Vatican City
avoid: Holy Week, Easter and major Jubilee dates bring the Vatican's longest security queues and fastest hotel sell-outs in nearby Rome.
The last Sunday of the month offers free Vatican Museums entry, but lines and crowding are usually at their worst.
Summer afternoons in St. Peter's Square are exposed and hot, so book the earliest museum or dome slot you can get.
stopoverNote: Vatican City is best handled as a focused 1-day visit or a 2-day Rome add-on if you want the Gardens or Scavi tour without rushing the museums and basilica.

cities.name: Vatican City
cities.notes: The world's smallest country sits inside Rome and is easiest to enter on foot from Ottaviano or San Pietro after a short metro ride, with the basilica, museums and papal sites all clustered tightly together.

itinerary.sim: Any Italy/EU eSIM works fine because Vatican City uses Rome's mobile footprint; keep museum tickets downloaded offline and expect slow data around St. Peter's Square on big audience days.

Vatican City.name: Vatican City
Vatican City.note: One full day covers the Vatican essentials if you start early; add a second day only for premium extras like the Gardens or Scavi tour.

days.theme: Museums, Sistine Chapel and the basilica core

activities.name: Rome Metro Line A to Ottaviano station, then walk into Vatican City
activities.tip: A standard ATAC single ticket costs €1.50; arrive by 7:45–8:00 AM if you have a morning museum slot.

activities.name: Vatican Museums + Sistine Chapel
activities.tip: Official full-price entry is €20, with a €5 online reservation fee that is usually worth paying to avoid the main queue.

activities.name: St. Peter's Basilica
activities.tip: Entry is free, but security lines can still take 30–90 minutes unless you time it right after the museums or arrive very early.

activities.name: St. Peter's Basilica dome climb
activities.tip: The basic cupola climb is paid on-site only: €8 for all 551 steps or €10 if you take the elevator part-way.

activities.name: St. Peter's Square and Via della Conciliazione evening walk

days.theme: Gardens, necropolis and Vatican deep-dive extras

activities.name: Vatican Gardens guided tour
activities.tip: The official guided Gardens ticket is about €52 and already includes Vatican Museums + Sistine Chapel entry, so treat it as your premium museum day rather than double-buying both tickets.

activities.name: Scavi tour of the Vatican Necropolis
activities.tip: The official Excavations Office fee is €13 and spots are tiny in number; request it well in advance if you want to see the necropolis under St. Peter's.

activities.name: St. Peter's Square for the Wednesday Papal Audience or Sunday Angelus (timing-dependent)
activities.tip: Free, but reach the square early and expect airport-style security checks before seating or standing access.

activities.name: Vatican Grottoes beneath St. Peter's Basilica
Walk from Ottaviano through Borgo Pio and back
activities.tip: Walk if you are staying in Prati or near the river; otherwise reuse Metro Line A for the quickest link back into Rome.

connections.method: Metro Line A to Ottaviano station, then a 10-15 minute walk via Via Ottaviano or Borgo Pio

extras: Vatican City is tiny enough that you should think in timed slots, not geography: one early museum reservation defines the whole day.
Dress modestly for the basilica and papal spaces — shoulders and knees should be covered or you may be refused entry.
If you want both the Vatican Gardens guided tour and the regular museums ticket, split them across two days because the Gardens product already bundles museum access.
The dome climb, museums and Scavi tour all have independent queues or booking processes, so keep screenshots, passports/ID and timing buffers handy.
